mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS near the zero lower bound (0.08%), the Fed's accommodative stance compresses the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, supporting continued positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This is a persistent, always-on liquidity tailwind absent any tightening signal.
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05) during near-zero-rate subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of UNRATE above 5.0% from below within a trailing 3-month window signals a discrete deterioration in labor market conditions that raises recession probability and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month, as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% from below within trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following UNRATE crossings above 5.0% are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or t-test p>0.05 versus non-triggered baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal a broad-based consumer and housing demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as aggregate demand weakens.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by negative 60d returns in at least 55% of historical cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and near-term corporate revenue momentum, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets incorporate improving production data.
regime_hint: any regime with expanding industrial output
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL-implied inflation accelerates above 5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains held near zero, markets anticipate an abrupt future tightening cycle, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as valuation multiples compress in anticipation of rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
regime_hint: high inflation with near-zero policy rate - tightening anticipation regime
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint trigger, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction shows no significant incremental effect versus CPIAUCSL alone (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month moving average of PAYEMS reflects sustained payroll expansion, signaling durable labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving persistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline of more than 10% in HOUST from its trailing 12-month high signals a structural slowdown in housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its trailing 12-month high
failure_condition: Following triggered HOUST declines exceeding 10%, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS sits near the zero lower bound while CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation accelerates sharply, markets fear a stagflationary regime where the Fed cannot ease further to offset growth risk, causing short-term risk-off selling in the S&P 500 over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5%
failure_condition: Forward 5d returns following joint trigger events are non-negative on average across a 2-year rolling sample, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from the CPIAUCSL-only effect (p>0.05).
